steps.args: {'prompt': "Extract all details relevant to Zillow's acquisition strategy, what Spruce does, strategic rationale, close date, integration, and housing super app. Quote key language. {{spruce_release}}"}
steps.result:
## Zillow’s acquisition of Spruce

### Transaction and close date
- Zillow Group **completed its acquisition of Spruce on September 11, 2023**.
- The original announcement, published **August 10, 2023**, said Zillow had entered into an agreement to acquire Spruce; it was updated after the transaction closed.
- The release does **not disclose financial terms**.

> “Zillow Group has completed its acquisition of Spruce, a tech-enabled title and escrow company.”

## What Spruce does
- Founded in **2016**, Spruce is a **tech-enabled title and escrow company**.
- It developed technology and operations supporting:
  - Title insurance
  - Closing coordination
  - Escrow
  - Recording services
- It provides these services nationwide and has served high-volume and innovative real estate companies.
- Its model combines **proprietary software** with experienced title and closing professionals.

> “Spruce built technology and operations to power title insurance, coordination, escrow, and recording services nationwide for some of the most innovative real estate companies.”

> “Pairing its proprietary software with a team of seasoned experts, Spruce emerged as a leader within the title and closing ecosystem, offering a seamless end-to-end experience for its clients.”

## Strategic rationale
The acquisition is intended to modernize the closing stage of a real estate transaction and strengthen Zillow’s broader effort to provide an end-to-end home-buying experience.

Key reasons include:

1. **Improve a critical, often-fragmented part of the transaction**
   - Zillow says helping more people move requires improvements throughout the real estate process, particularly closing.

   > “Making it easier for people to move requires improvements across the real estate transaction — including the critical closing process.”

2. **Build a digital closing-services offering**
   - Zillow and Spruce plan to create a technology-powered closing product for Zillow customers.

   > “Over time, Zillow Group and Spruce will build a digital-powered closing services offering for Zillow customers.”

3. **Acquire proven title and transaction expertise**
   - Zillow highlighted Spruce’s team, technology and history serving high-volume clients.

   > “This acquisition is driven by the expertise and skill the Spruce team has when it comes to creating solutions for faster and effortless real estate transactions.”

4. **Create a technology-forward, high-volume title operation**
   
   > “With Spruce, Zillow is building a tech-forward, high volume title company for the future.”

5. **Replace Zillow’s prior closing-services approach**
   - Zillow had previously announced it was winding down its existing closing-services division to pursue a more technology-oriented solution.

   > “Previously Zillow announced it is sunsetting their existing closing services division in favor of exploring a more tech-forward solution.”

6. **Advance Zillow’s product-by-product acquisition and expansion strategy**
   - Spruce is characterized as a strategic building block rather than a standalone offering.

   > “The acquisition is a strategic move in Zillow’s efforts to steadily roll out products and services to create a seamless end-to-end home-buying experience for customers and partners.”

## Integration plans
- Spruce’s team will join Zillow Group.
- The two teams will jointly develop a **new customer-facing closing offering**.
- Zillow cautioned that development and rollout will take time; no specific product-launch date was provided.

> “When the Spruce team joins Zillow Group after the closing of this acquisition, the two teams will work together to create this new customer-facing offering.”

> “It will take time to develop and eventually roll out a new closing solution for Zillow customers.”

The expected result is a modernized closing process:

> “Zillow and Spruce will power a modern closing experience.”

## Role in Zillow’s “housing super app”
Spruce is described as a **building block** and the planned closing service as a **key component** of Zillow’s housing super app.

Zillow defines the housing super app as a platform that connects the currently fragmented stages of moving into one coordinated transaction:

> “We expect it to be a key component of Zillow’s housing super app, which will connect all the fragmented pieces of moving into one streamlined transaction.”

Accordingly, Spruce adds title, escrow and closing capabilities to Zillow’s attempt to deliver a seamless, end-to-end home-buying journey for consumers and partners.

## Management’s stated vision
Zillow’s Matt Daimler emphasized removing title-related friction:

> “Title should never be a blocker to a great transaction and our goal is for our customers to see and feel how much better the real estate transaction can be when they work with Zillow.”

Spruce CEO and co-founder Patrick Burns framed the combination around improving the consumer transaction:

> “Like Zillow, Spruce is driven by the vision of offering an amazing transaction experience to buyers and sellers.”

> “We’re excited to join Zillow in the ambitious mission to create the best closing solution for the modern home-buying process.”

steps.args: {'prompt': 'Extract all details about VSAI acquisition: close date, capabilities, strategic rationale, intended Zillow product integration and monetization. {{vsai_release}}'}
steps.result:
## VSAI acquisition details

### Close date
- **Acquisition closed:** **October 7, 2024**
- Announced by Zillow on October 8, 2024.
- Financial terms were **not disclosed**.
- VSAI’s team began integrating into Zillow following the close.

### VSAI capabilities
- AI-powered **virtual staging** for listing photographs, eliminating the need for physical furniture and décor.
- Users can:
  - Upload photographs of empty rooms.
  - Select the room type.
  - Choose a furniture style.
  - Generate high-quality, fully staged images within seconds.
  - Interactively edit images and quickly implement changes.
- Zillow says the generated images are **nearly indistinguishable from traditionally staged listings**.
- Intended users include **home sellers, real estate agents, and photographers**.
- At the announcement date, the technology remained available to real estate professionals through **VSAI’s website**.

### Strategic rationale
- Elevate the quality and interactivity of listings on Zillow.
- Give home shoppers a more realistic and accurate sense of a property before visiting it.
- Expand Zillow’s suite of software and advertising solutions for real estate professionals.
- Support Zillow’s broader goal of making real estate more:
  - Digital
  - Integrated
  - Productive
- Reduce staging delays and revision cycles, allowing professionals to spend more time on high-value, person-to-person client service.
- Add another interactive-media option for sellers. Zillow cited its 2024 Consumer Housing Trends Report, which found that **71% of sellers are more likely to hire an agent who offers virtual tours and/or interactive floor plans**.
- Strengthen Zillow’s broader digital transformation and AI capabilities.

### Intended Zillow product integration
- Zillow is working to integrate VSAI into **Zillow Showcase**.
- The intended result is a more immersive listing experience that helps:
  - Agents market homes.
  - Sellers present properties effectively.
  - Buyers understand a home before stepping inside.
- Zillow also plans to make the technology available to **agents and photographers through Zillow’s product suite**, though no specific launch date was provided.
- Zillow noted that Showcase listings sell **faster and for more money**, without providing supporting figures in the announcement.

### Monetization
- The announcement does **not disclose a specific monetization model**, pricing, subscription structure, or incremental revenue target.
- Zillow positions VSAI as part of its broader **software and advertising solutions** for real estate professionals.
- The clearest intended commercial path is integration into **Zillow Showcase** and Zillow’s professional product suite, potentially strengthening the value of those offerings to agents and photographers.
- The article does not state whether virtual staging will be:
  - Included in an existing Showcase package,
  - Sold as a paid add-on,
  - Offered through a separate subscription, or
  - Used primarily to increase adoption and retention of Zillow’s existing professional products.

### Team and background
- VSAI was co-founded in **2023** by **Michael Bonacina and Mikhail Andreev**.
- The founders began developing the technology at **Harvard Innovation Labs** while researching AI and image processing.
- Most of the VSAI team is based in **Munich**.
- As part of the acquisition, the team joined Zillow’s **AI and technology teams** to continue developing the virtual-staging product.

steps.result:
## Aryeo acquisition

- Zillow announced ahead of its Q2 2023 earnings report that it had **acquired Aryeo**, a real-estate software and content-management platform.
- Aryeo’s platform supports **real-estate photography and media businesses across the U.S.**
- Zillow said Aryeo would **complement and enhance its ShowingTime+ product suite**.
- The article does **not disclose** the purchase price, deal terms, closing date, Aryeo’s revenue, or expected financial contribution.  
- The source misspells the company once as “Areyo,” but otherwise identifies it as Aryeo.

## Q2 2023 revenue mix and growth

| Metric/segment | Q2 2023 result | Growth/comparison |
|---|---:|---:|
| **Total revenue** | **$506 million** | Up $37 million from Q1 2023, implying Q1 revenue of $469 million; up slightly from $504 million in Q2 2022, or approximately **0.4% YoY** |
| **Residential revenue** | Dollar amount not provided | **Down 3% YoY**; nevertheless at the upper end of Zillow’s outlook |
| **Mortgage revenue** | Dollar amount not provided | **Down 17% YoY**, as elevated interest rates suppressed mortgage applications |
| **Rentals revenue** | Dollar amount not provided | **Up 28% YoY**, making rentals the principal growth highlight |

Additional revenue observations:

- Total revenue landed in the **upper portion of Zillow’s outlook range** and exceeded many forecasters’ expectations.
- Zillow attributed residential revenue’s better-than-expected performance to **more connections with Premier Agents than forecast**.
- The article does not provide segment revenue dollars or percentages of total revenue, so a precise revenue-mix calculation is not possible.

## Zillow’s stated strategy and related actions

CEO Rich Barton said Zillow was focused on:

- **Improving and integrating customer and partner experiences**
- Making particular progress in:
  - **Touring**
  - **Financing**
  - **Renting**
- Continuing to outperform the broader real-estate industry despite a difficult housing market.

Specific strategic moves cited:

1. **Expand ShowingTime+**
   - Zillow had been investing heavily in ShowingTime+ products during 2023.
   - It was adding features and expanding the suite into additional markets.
   - The Aryeo acquisition was intended to strengthen this offering through photography, media, software, and content-management capabilities.

2. **Scale Listing Showcase**
   - Listing Showcase was described as a newer **subscription-based product**.
   - It had been made available to more agents in:
     - Seattle
     - San Diego
     - Los Angeles
     - Chicago
     - Atlanta
   - ShowingTime+ planned to add several more markets in September 2023 and continue expanding through year-end.

3. **Strengthen listing distribution through partnerships**
   - On August 1, 2023, Zillow announced a partnership with **Redfin** to share **new-construction single-family-home listings**.

4. **Drive Premier Agent connections**
   - Higher-than-forecast connections with Premier Agents supported residential revenue, indicating continued emphasis on connecting consumers with agent partners.

5. **Lean into rentals as a growth area**
   - Rentals revenue grew 28% year over year, aligning with management’s stated focus on improving the renting experience.

steps.args: {'prompt': 'Extract full-year 2024 and 2023 revenue by category (Residential, Rentals, Mortgages, total), growth rates, stated strategy, and references to Follow Up Boss, ShowingTime+, Showcase, Rentals and Home Loans. Explain what drove evolving revenue mix. {{fy2024_letter}}'}
steps.result:
## Full-year revenue

**Zillow Group revenue, $ millions**

| Category | 2024 | 2023 | YoY growth | Share of total, 2024 | Share of total, 2023 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Residential | **$1,594M** | **$1,452M** | **10%** | 71.3% | 74.7% |
| Rentals | **$453M** | **$357M** | **27%** | 20.3% | 18.4% |
| Mortgages | **$145M** | **$96M** | **51%** | 6.5% | 4.9% |
| **Total revenue** | **$2,236M** | **$1,945M** | **15%** | 100% | 100% |
| Other/reconciliation | $44M | $40M | 10% | 2.0% | 2.1% |

*The letter rounds Residential to **$1.6 billion**, For Sale to **$1.7 billion**, and total revenue to **$2.2 billion**. “For Sale” combines Residential and Mortgages: approximately **$1.739 billion in 2024**, up about **12%** from $1.548 billion in 2023. Total revenue also includes a small amount of “Other” revenue, so the three requested categories do not add exactly to total revenue.*

## Stated strategy

Zillow described its strategy as a **differentiated housing super app** built around its large residential-real-estate audience. Its central objective is to convert more people already shopping on Zillow into completed transactions and earn more revenue at multiple stages of each move.

The principal elements were:

1. **Create an integrated, technology-enabled moving experience** covering agent selection, touring, selling, financing, listings, title and closing.
2. **Connect high-intent consumers with high-performing agents** and improve conversion through Real Time Touring, better routing and agent software.
3. **Integrate Premier Agent with Zillow Home Loans**, increasing the number of customers using both agent and mortgage services.
4. **Expand seller services**, especially Zillow Showcase.
5. **Increase revenue per transaction** through buyer and seller referral fees, mortgages, Showcase, and title/closing services.
6. **Scale Enhanced Markets using a “land-and-expand” model.** These markets accounted for 21% of connections in Q4 2024; Zillow expected more than 35% by the end of 2025 and, over time, 75% of Zillow transactions.
7. **Scale Rentals**, particularly multifamily advertising, against what Zillow estimates is a $25 billion addressable market.

Management also stated a longer-term path to **$5 billion of revenue and a 45% Adjusted EBITDA margin in a normalized housing market**, while targeting positive GAAP net income in 2025.

## Product and business references

### Follow Up Boss

- Acquired at the end of 2023.
- Enables agents and customers to communicate and collaborate in the Zillow app.
- Zillow integrated more of its data into Follow Up Boss so agents can identify higher-intent buyers and sellers.
- In Enhanced Markets, **more than 80% of connections** were being managed through Follow Up Boss.
- Follow Up Boss was specifically identified as a contributor to 2024 Residential revenue growth.

### ShowingTime+

- Listed, alongside Follow Up Boss and the New Construction marketplace, as a contributor to full-year Residential revenue.
- Its tools support agents and sellers through touring, listing presentation and related software.
- The letter’s Showcase performance reference directs readers to **showingtimeplus.com/showcase-facts**.

### Zillow Showcase

- An AI-powered listing product featuring rich media and floor plans that also elevates agents’ brands.
- Expanded to **1.7% of all new U.S. for-sale listings** by year-end 2024.
- Zillow’s intermediate-term objective is **5%–10% of U.S. listings**, representing an estimated **$150 million–$300 million revenue opportunity**.
- Zillow said Showcase listings sell faster and for more money than comparable non-Showcase listings and help agents win approximately **30% more listings**.
- Showcase expansion was cited as one of the primary drivers of Residential revenue growth.
- Zillow invested in the Showcase sales team to support future growth.

### Rentals

- Full-year revenue grew **27% to $453 million**, considerably faster than Residential.
- The main driver was **multifamily revenue, up 42%** for the year.
- Multifamily properties advertising on Zillow increased from **37,000 to 50,000**.
- Total active rental listings increased **15% to 1.9 million**.
- Zillow reported an average of **29 million monthly unique Rentals visitors** in Q4.
- Growth was supported by the Realtor.com multifamily-listing distribution partnership and a multifamily advertising campaign launched in 2024.
- Zillow also announced a Redfin partnership to distribute Zillow’s multifamily listings.
- Management characterized Rentals as a potential **$1 billion-plus revenue business**.

### Zillow Home Loans / Mortgages

- Full-year Mortgages revenue rose **51% to $145 million**, the fastest growth among the reported categories.
- Purchase mortgage origination volume increased **102% to $3.1 billion** for the year.
- Zillow attributed this to better integration between **Zillow Home Loans and Premier Agent**, plus stronger engagement on its apps and sites.
- In mature Enhanced Markets, customer adoption of both a Premier Agent partner and Zillow Home Loans reached the **mid-teens**, versus 6% in Q1 2023.
- Buyers connected with both offerings transacted through Zillow at an **80% higher rate** than buyers connected only with a Premier Agent partner.
- Q4 Mortgages revenue increased **86%**, supported by a **90%** increase in purchase-loan originations.

## What drove the evolving revenue mix?

Zillow’s revenue mix shifted modestly away from its traditional Residential business toward Rentals and Mortgages:

- Residential declined from about **74.7% of revenue in 2023 to 71.3% in 2024**.
- Rentals increased from **18.4% to 20.3%**.
- Mortgages increased from **4.9% to 6.5%**.

This did **not** reflect a decline in Residential—Residential still grew 10%. Rather, the newer or less mature businesses grew much faster:

- **Residential:** +$142 million
- **Rentals:** +$96 million
- **Mortgages:** +$49 million
- **Other:** +$4 million
- **Total increase:** +$291 million

The mix evolution was driven by:

1. **Multifamily Rentals expansion:** more advertised properties, more listings, greater distribution and higher traffic drove 42% multifamily revenue growth.
2. **Mortgage integration:** combining Premier Agent and Zillow Home Loans increased adoption, conversion and purchase-loan originations.
3. **Residential conversion improvements:** better routing, Real Time Touring, Enhanced Markets and Follow Up Boss helped Zillow outperform the housing market.
4. **Seller-product monetization:** Showcase added a new revenue stream tied to listings rather than only buyer-agent connections.
5. **Broader monetization of each move:** Zillow is adding financing, seller referrals, listing products, title and closing services, increasing potential revenue per transaction.
6. **Follow Up Boss acquisition effect:** because it was acquired at the end of 2023, 2024 included a much fuller revenue contribution.

Thus, Residential remained the core business, but Rentals and Mortgages grew faster and became more meaningful components of revenue—consistent with Zillow’s shift from a primarily lead-generation model toward a broader, integrated transaction and moving-services platform.

**Source:** Zillow Group, *Fourth Quarter 2024 Shareholder Letter*, published February 11, 2025 on MarketScreener—strategy and product discussion on pp. 1–5; full-year highlights and category results on pp. 6–8.

{'final_result': '## Answer

As of December 2024, Zillow’s acquisition strategy over the prior two years was a **capability-led buildout of its “housing super app,” not a return to capital-intensive home buying**. From December 2022 through October 2024, Zillow acquired businesses that fill specific gaps in the for-sale transaction workflow—listing media, agent software, title/closing, and AI—while its faster-growing Rentals and Mortgages businesses were expanded mainly through organic investment, integration, and distribution partnerships.

### Acquisitions during the two-year period

| Date | Acquisition | Capability acquired | Strategic role |
|---|---|---|---|
| Dec. 2022 | **VRX Media** | Nationwide photographer network; drone and HD photography, 3D tours, virtual staging and media delivery | Supplied the service infrastructure behind ShowingTime+’s Listing Media Services and Zillow Showcase, improving listing quality and creating agent-facing products beyond traditional lead generation. Terms were not disclosed. |
| Aug. 2023 | **Aryeo** | Workflow and content-management software for real-estate photographers and media companies | Complemented VRX and ShowingTime+ by adding the software layer needed to create, manage and distribute rich listing media. Reported consideration was approximately **$35 million**. |
| Sept. 2023 | **Spruce** | Tech-enabled title, escrow, closing coordination and recording | Added a missing transaction-stage capability. Zillow described it as a building block for a digital closing offering and a streamlined end-to-end home-buying experience. The transaction was approximately **$24 million gross**, or about **$19 million net of acquired cash**. |
| Dec. 2023 | **Follow Up Boss** | Customer-relationship-management software for agents and teams | This was the largest and most strategically significant purchase: **$400 million initial cash consideration plus up to $100 million of earnout**. It improves agents’ management and conversion of Zillow consumer connections and also brings recurring software revenue. Zillow kept it available as a standalone, open platform rather than restricting it to Premier Agent customers. |
| Oct. 2024 | **Virtual Staging AI (VSAI)** | Generative-AI virtual staging of listing photos | A small technology/talent acquisition intended for integration into Zillow Showcase and Zillow’s agent/photographer product suite, making listings more engaging and the media-production process faster. Terms were not disclosed. |

### What this pattern says about the strategy

The transactions were **vertically connected rather than diversified acquisitions**:

1. **Own more of the digital workflow without owning the house.** After winding down Zillow Offers, Zillow bought software, data/workflow and service capabilities that increase monetization per customer while avoiding iBuying’s inventory and housing-price risk.
2. **Build an integrated agent-and-consumer platform.** VRX, Aryeo and VSAI improve how a home is marketed; Follow Up Boss helps agents convert and manage customer relationships; Spruce addresses the closing stage.
3. **Add recurring and transaction-adjacent revenue.** Follow Up Boss and ShowingTime+/Showcase broaden Zillow beyond Premier Agent lead advertising toward CRM subscriptions, listing products and eventually title/closing fees.
4. **Prefer targeted tuck-ins, except where scale matters.** Four transactions were relatively small or undisclosed capability acquisitions; Follow Up Boss was the major platform purchase because CRM is central to conversion and agent workflow.

## Alignment with Zillow’s evolving revenue mix

Zillow’s 2024 revenue mix illustrates both the fit and the limits of the acquisition program:

| Revenue category | 2023 | 2024 | Growth | Share of total: 2023 → 2024 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Residential | $1.452B | $1.594B | 9.78% | 74.65% → 71.29% |
| Rentals | $357M | $453M | 26.89% | 18.35% → 20.26% |
| Mortgages | $96M | $145M | 51.04% | 4.94% → 6.48% |
| Other | $40M | $44M | 10.00% | 2.06% → 1.97% |
| **Total** | **$1.945B** | **$2.236B** | **14.96%** | **100%** |

### Direct alignment: strengthening and broadening Residential

The acquisitions largely support **Residential**, Zillow’s core but slower-growing category. Follow Up Boss, ShowingTime+, Zillow Showcase and new construction were identified as contributors to 2024 Residential revenue. Follow Up Boss also supports Zillow’s Enhanced Markets model: by year-end 2024, more than 80% of connections in those markets were managed through the CRM.

VRX, Aryeo and VSAI collectively enable Zillow Showcase—an agent-facing, rich-media listing product that represented about **1.7% of new U.S. for-sale listings** at year-end 2024. Zillow estimated that reaching 5%–10% penetration could represent a **$150 million–$300 million revenue opportunity**. Thus, the acquisitions help Residential evolve from primarily buyer-lead monetization toward a mix that also includes seller/listing products and software subscriptions.

Spruce aligns with the same objective but was still more of a future monetization asset as of December 2024: it potentially adds title and closing revenue and allows Zillow to earn more from each completed transaction.

### Indirect alignment: better conversion into Mortgages

Although Zillow did not make a major mortgage acquisition during this period, Follow Up Boss and the broader integrated transaction workflow help route and convert high-intent customers into **Zillow Home Loans**. Mortgages revenue rose **51.04% to $145 million**, and purchase-loan origination volume increased 102% to $3.1 billion in 2024. Customers connected with both a Premier Agent partner and Zillow Home Loans reportedly transacted at an 80% higher rate than those connected only with an agent.

Thus, the acquisition strategy supports mortgage growth by improving customer and agent conversion, even though the lending capacity itself was primarily built organically.

### Important contrast: Rentals became larger without rental-focused M&A

Rentals revenue increased **26.89% to $453 million** and rose to **20.26% of total revenue**, yet none of these five acquisitions was principally a rental acquisition. Rental growth came mainly from organic multifamily expansion, advertising investment and listing-distribution partnerships; multifamily revenue grew 42% in 2024.

This distinction is important: **M&A was concentrated on completing and monetizing the for-sale journey, while revenue diversification toward Rentals and Mortgages was largely organic.** Residential still grew, but Rentals and Mortgages grew faster, reducing Residential’s share by 3.36 percentage points despite the acquired contributions.

## Conclusion

As of December 2024, Zillow was using acquisitions to assemble a **capital-light, end-to-end real-estate technology and services stack**: market the listing, manage the lead, finance the buyer and eventually close the transaction. The strategy aligns with the evolving revenue mix by:

- protecting and diversifying the still-dominant Residential franchise through software and seller products;
- increasing conversion and revenue per transaction, which supports rapid Mortgage growth; and
- complementing, rather than directly causing, the broader mix shift toward faster-growing Rentals and Mortgages.

In short, Zillow’s M&A strategy was less about purchasing near-term revenue in every segment and more about acquiring the connective technology needed to monetize a larger portion of each customer’s moving journey.
